title: Churros with Chocolate Sauce

seo.title: Churros with Chocolate Sauce: Authentic Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make delicious Churros with Chocolate Sauce at home! This easy, authentic Mexican recipe brings the taste of Mexico to your kitchen. Perfect for dessert!

fried dough with chocolate dip recipe
seo.h1: Churros with Chocolate Sauce: The Ultimate Mexican Dessert
h2Headings: What are Churros with Chocolate Sauce?
Ingredients You Need for Homemade Churros
Pro Tips for the Best Churros with Chocolate Sauce
seo.imageAlt: Delicious homemade Churros with Chocolate Sauce, a classic Mexican dessert perfect for sharing.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the crispy perfection of homemade Churros, served warm with a luscious chocolate dipping sauce – a true taste of Mexico!

description: Indulge in the crispy, golden delight of authentic Mexican Churros, perfectly fried and coated in cinnamon sugar. Served with a rich, velvety chocolate dipping sauce, this dessert is a celebration of flavor and tradition. A beloved treat that promises a sweet escape to the heart of Mexico with every delicious bite.

ingredients.notes: such as Ibarra or Abuelita, chopped

instructions.title: Prepare the Churro Dough
instructions.text: In a medium saucepan, combine 1 cup water, 1/2 cup butter, 2 tablespoons granulated sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil over medium heat. Remove from heat and immediately stir in 1 cup all-purpose flour until a thick dough forms and pulls away from the sides of the pan. Let it cool for 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Incorporate the Eggs
instructions.text: Transfer the slightly cooled dough to a large mixing bowl. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition until fully incorporated and the dough is smooth and glossy. This can be done with a stand mixer or by hand with a sturdy spoon.

instructions.title: Make the Cinnamon Sugar Coating
instructions.text: In a shallow dish, combine 1/2 cup granulated sugar and 1 tablespoon ground cinnamon. Mix well and set aside. This will be used to coat the churros after frying.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chocolate Sauce
instructions.text: In a separate saucepan, combine the chopped Mexican chocolate, 1 1/2 cups whole milk, and the cinnamon stick. Heat over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the chocolate is fully melted and the sauce is smooth and slightly thickened. Remove the cinnamon stick and stir in the vanilla extract. Keep warm.

instructions.title: Heat Oil and Pipe Churros
instructions.text: Pour vegetable oil into a deep pot or Dutch oven, enough to reach about 3 inches deep. Heat the oil to 375F (190C). While the oil heats, transfer the churro dough to a piping bag fitted with a large star tip (like an Ateco 826 or 827).

instructions.title: Fry the Churros
instructions.text: Carefully pipe 4-6 inch strips of dough directly into the hot oil, using kitchen shears or a knife to cut the dough. Do not overcrowd the pot. Fry for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through. Use tongs to remove the churros and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Coat and Serve
instructions.text: While the churros are still warm, roll them in the prepared cinnamon sugar mixture, ensuring they are evenly coated. Arrange the coated churros on a serving platter alongside the warm chocolate dipping sauce. Serve immediately and enjoy these delightful Mexican treats!

tips: Maintain the oil temperature: Use a thermometer to keep the oil at 375F (190C). Too cool, and churros will be greasy; too hot, and they will burn outside before cooking inside.
For the best churro texture, ensure your piping bag has a strong star tip. This creates the ridges that give churros their characteristic crispiness and help them cook evenly.
Mexican chocolate is key for an authentic sauce. If unavailable, use unsweetened cocoa powder and adjust sugar to taste, adding a pinch of cayenne for a subtle kick.
article: Churros with Chocolate Sauce is a beloved Mexican Desserts dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
